title: Thlaspi glastifolium
intro: Thlaspi glastifolium, also known as Field Pennycress, is a small, annual plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its clusters of small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Thlaspi glastifolium include glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thlaspi glastifolium is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.

This family is a large and diverse group of plants, which also includes species of cabbage, broccoli, and kale.
